folio.label: 202v
folio.l: The hakeneye eke , æat his ©eman rod vp oon
So swatte , that vnethe mygút it gon
A male tweyfold , vp on his croper lay
It semed that he caried , litel array
Al ligút for somer , rod this worthy man
And in myn herte , to wondre I began
What that he was , til that I vnderstode
How æ his cloke , was sowed to his hode
For whicú , whan I had longe a vysed me
I demed hi~ som Chanou~ , for to be
His hat heng at his bak , doun by a lace
For he had ryden more , than trot or pace
He had ay , priked , lyke as he were wood
A clote leef he hadde , vnder his hood
For swete , and for to kepe , his hed from hete
But it was ioye , for to sen hi~ swete
His forhe dropped , as a stillatorie
Were ful of plaunteyn , and of ¹itorie
And whan æ he was come , he gan to crie
God saue quod he , this ioly compaignye
Fast haue I pryked q¶ he , for ©oure sake
By cause , that I wolde , ©ow a take
To ryden in the same , myrie compaignye
His ©eman was eke , ful of curteisie
And seide sirx x now in the morwe tyde
Out of ©oure hostelrie , I saw ©ow ride
And warned heere my lord , & my souäayn
Whicú that to riden witú ©ow , is ful fayn
For his disport , he louetú daliaunce
¢ Frend for thy warnynge , god ©eue the good chaunce

note.notetxt: 40Indication in the left margin for a paragraph mark to be included. But the paraph was not executed.MP

folio.l: Tha~ne seide oure hoost , for certeyn it wolde seme
Thy lord were wys , and so I may wel deme
He is ful iocunde , also dar I leye
Can he ougút telle , a mery tale or tweye
Witú whicú he glade may , this compaignye
Who sire my lord , ©a ©a witú outen lye
He can of myrthe , and eke of iolytee
Nat but I now , also sire trustetú me
And ©e hi~ knew , as wel as do I
ñe wolde wondre , how wel and craftily
He coude werke , and that in sondry wyse
